MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy D.F. Barteau, Esq. of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-2022), appearing for defendant / moving party Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Leroy Cabral

RULING:
Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cabral was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to address any of plaintiff's work locations other than Standard Oil in its separate statement precludes the invocation of the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party

RULING:
Defendant Pacific Mechanical Corp.'s motion for summary judgment. No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to plaintiff's first (negligence), second (strict liability) and fifth (contractor liability) causes of action and granted as to plaintiff's third cause of action (false representation). By failing to present plaintiff's responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Because plaintiff did not have the opportunity to respond to the evidentiary materials submitted with the reply papers, the court declines to consider those materials. Plaintiff concedes that his claim for false representation should be dismissed. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
